title: Why Being Too Sure Can Be Scary
summary: A TV and radio host shares his thoughts after talking to many people for over 50 years. He says that today, people are too certain about their opinions and don't listen to others. In the past, radio shows were more formal, but now, phone-in shows let ordinary people share their views. The host notices that arguments have become more angry and divided, especially after events like Brexit. People often dislike those who disagree with them, making it hard to have friendly talks. He warns that being too sure can stop us from understanding different ideas and getting along.

background_read: This article is about how people talk to each other in debates. A host who works on TV and radio has noticed that over many years, conversations have become more argumentative and less friendly. He explains that in the past, radio was more formal, but now shows allow everyday people to call in. Events like Brexit have made people more divided, and they often don't like others with different opinions. The host thinks being too sure of your own ideas can be scary because it stops understanding.
Article_Structure: WHO: A TV and radio host who has talked to many people for over 50 years.
WHAT: He says people are too certain and don't listen, making arguments angry and divided.
WHERE: In the UK, especially on radio shows and during events like Brexit.
WHY: To warn that being too sure can harm understanding and friendships.

perspectives.description: The host believes certainty leads to division and less listening in society.
